commit.message:
chore: release v0.4.7

Add --verbose / -v flag to the backup subcommand so users can run
`omega-backup backup --verbose` (previously required the global
flag to be placed before the subcommand: `omega-backup -v backup`).
When active, borg's --list output and all newline-terminated stderr
(stats block, warnings) are printed live.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(agent-core): update datetime tests to match user-message injection

The implementation injects datetime into the user message (not
system_instruction) to keep system_instruction stable for Gemini
context caching. Update tests to check prompt history accordingly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(observability): instrument core orchestration paths with OTel spans

Add OpenTelemetry spans to all 9 core orchestration services per §10.13.2:
- task.transition in TransitionService
- task.assign in SchedulerService
- worker.prepare/worker.run in WorkerSupervisorService
- worker.heartbeat in HeartbeatService
- validation.run in ValidationRunnerService
- review.route in ReviewRouterService
- review.lead_decision in ReviewDecisionService
- merge.prepare/merge.execute in MergeExecutorService

Create span name and attribute key constants in @factory/observability.
Add 14 span verification tests. Re-export SpanStatusCode, Span, and
InMemorySpanExporter from the observability package.

feat(control-plane): implement artifact and review packet retrieval endpoints

Add REST endpoints for retrieving task artifacts, review history,
review cycle packets, parsed packet content, and merge details.

Endpoints:
- GET /tasks/:taskId/artifacts - artifact tree organized by type
- GET /tasks/:taskId/packets/:packetId - parsed JSON packet content
- GET /tasks/:taskId/reviews - review cycle history with decisions
- GET /tasks/:taskId/reviews/:cycleId/packets - specialist packets
- GET /tasks/:taskId/merge - merge queue item + validation runs

New files:
- review/artifacts.controller.ts + artifacts.service.ts
- review/reviews.controller.ts + reviews.service.ts
- merge/merge-details.controller.ts + merge-details.service.ts
- 6 test files with 31 new tests (3,268 total passing)

Closes T084.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix CD pipeline: wait for CI on version bump PR (#255)

## Summary
- CD has been unable to publish for ~11 hours due to a deadlock in the
version bump step
- `gh pr merge --admin` cannot bypass the required `build` status check
on newer GitHub rulesets
- Rush's bump commits contain `[skip ci]` which prevents CI from ever
running on the bump PR

## Fix
- Push an empty commit (without `[skip ci]`) to the bump branch to
trigger CI
- Wait for the `build` check to pass (10min timeout) before merging the
PR
- Removed `[skip ci]` from the PR title to avoid any skip behavior

## Version Reset
- Reset all lockstep package versions from 0.14.0 back to 0.13.0 (last
published to npm)
- Removed changelog entries for unpublished versions 0.13.1–0.14.0
- Created a single minor change file to represent all the unpublished
changes
- Cleaned up 3 stale `version/bump-*` branches from previous failed CD
runs

## Test plan
- [ ] Merge this PR and verify the next CD run with a real version bump
succeeds
- [ ] The existing change files include a `minor` bump, so the next
merge to main should trigger an actual publish

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: grackle
repository.description: Manage and orchestrate AI coding agents (Claude Code, Copilot, Goose, etc.) on remote machines — task decomposition, multi-agent coordination, and real-time visibility across environments.

commit.message:
Fix profile workdir: use local repo for Copilot boot, skip git ops

Copilot needs a repo with init.ps1 to emit 'Environment loaded:' —
using TEMP caused a 30min boot timeout. Now uses the local repo path
without any git fetch/checkout.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add profile to default WorkerTaskTypes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(randutil): add random value generation utilities (#607)

New package with 11 random utility functions:
- Int, Float64, Bool: basic random values
- Pick[T]: random element from slice
- Shuffle[T]: random permutation
- Sample[T]: random subset without replacement
- String, Hex, Alpha, AlphaNum: random string generators
- WeightedPick[T]: weighted random selection

All generic functions use math/rand/v2 for performance.
Includes doc.go, 25+ unit tests, 3 benchmarks, 3 examples.

Co-authored-by: Jordan <jordan@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(application): implement audit event query service (T074)

Add AuditQueryService with getEntityTimeline() and searchAuditEvents()
for querying the append-only audit log with flexible, combinable filters
and offset-based pagination.

- New AuditEventQueryPort (read-side, CQRS-lite separation from write port)
- AuditEventFilters: entityType, entityId, eventType, actorType, actorId, startTime, endTime
- PaginatedAuditResult with total count and page metadata
- Input validation (non-empty entity params, time range consistency)
- Pagination normalization (clamping, defaults)
- Infrastructure: findByEntityPaginated() and searchFiltered() with Drizzle dynamic queries
- Infrastructure: createAuditEventQueryPortAdapter() with shared mapRowToRecord()
- 26 unit tests covering timelines, filters, pagination, validation, edge cases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(discovery): add fuzz tests for registry operations (#606)

Add four fuzz targets:
- FuzzRegister: arbitrary service/ID/addr with sentinel error verification
- FuzzResolve: resolution with arbitrary service names
- FuzzSetStatus: status transitions with arbitrary inputs
- FuzzHeartbeat: heartbeat with arbitrary service/ID pairs

Co-authored-by: Jordan <jordan@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: implement Azure Incident Copilot MVP

Complete TypeScript monorepo with all 87 tasks across 9 phases:

- Shared package: types, validation, SQLite persistence (node:sqlite),
  log/job/result repositories, mock AI analysis adapter, seed data
- API server: Express REST API with incident CRUD, log submission,
  job tracking, analysis results, correlation IDs, CORS
- Worker: polling-based async processor with mock AI analysis
- Frontend: React + Vite with incident list, create, detail pages,
  log submission, analysis display, job status polling
- Tests: 67 tests (unit + API integration + E2E workflow)
- Scripts: dev.sh (concurrent startup), verify.sh (E2E verification)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(cond): add generic conditional and comparison utilities (#513)

New package with 10 generic utility functions:
- Ternary[T]: inline conditional expression
- Coalesce[T], CoalesceFunc[T]: first non-zero value
- Clamp[T]: restrict to range
- Min[T], Max[T]: variadic min/max
- Zero[T], IsZero[T]: zero value helpers
- Switch[T]: pattern matching with Case structs

All functions are generic and type-safe.
Includes doc.go, 25+ unit tests, 3 benchmarks, 4 examples.

Co-authored-by: Jordan <jordan@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(encoding): add base62 encode/decode utilities (#506)

Add a new encoding package with base62 encoding for compact, URL-safe
representations of binary data and integers.

New functions:
- Base62Encode — encode bytes to base62 string
- Base62Decode — decode base62 string to bytes
- Base62EncodeUint64 — encode uint64 to compact base62
- Base62DecodeUint64 — decode base62 string to uint64

Includes 14 tests, 2 benchmarks, and 2 fuzz targets.
100% statement coverage.

Co-authored-by: Jordan <jordan@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(application): implement rework loop with rejection context (T062)

Add ReworkContextService that assembles a validated RejectionContext from
persisted review data (LeadReviewDecision + specialist ReviewPackets +
DevResultPacket) for inclusion in rework TaskPackets.

New files:
- ports/rework-context.ports.ts — Port interfaces for querying rejected
  review data
- services/rework-context.service.ts — Service that builds RejectionContext
  at query time from already-persisted data
- services/rework-context.service.test.ts — 22 tests covering happy path,
  fallbacks, error handling, and defensive parsing

Design: RejectionContext is derived from existing records rather than stored
redundantly, keeping data normalized and avoiding schema migrations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: refresh business roadmap reality check

feat(convert): add safe type conversion utilities (#502)

New package with 13 conversion functions:
- ToInt, ToInt64, ToFloat64, ToUint: string-to-number with fallback
- ToBool: flexible boolean parsing (true/1/yes/on)
- ToString: any-to-string conversion
- ToDuration: string-to-Duration with fallback
- MustInt, MustFloat64: panic-on-error conversions
- Ptr[T]: create pointer to value
- Deref[T], DerefOr[T]: safe pointer dereferencing

All functions return sensible defaults instead of errors.
Includes doc.go, 25+ unit tests, 3 benchmarks, 4 examples.

Co-authored-by: Jordan <jordan@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(stringutil): add string utility functions (#496)

New package with 8 string utilities:
- ToKebabCase, ToPascalCase: case conversion
- PadLeft, PadRight: string padding
- IsBlank: empty/whitespace check
- Reverse: rune-aware string reversal
- WordWrap: line wrapping at word boundaries
- CountWords: word counting

All functions are Unicode-aware (rune-based, not byte-based).
Includes doc.go, 20+ unit tests, 3 benchmarks, 1 fuzz, 3+ examples.

Co-authored-by: Jordan <jordan@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(maputil): add generic map utility functions (#487)

New package with 9 generic map utilities:
- Keys, Values: extract keys/values as slices
- Merge: combine multiple maps (last-write-wins)
- Pick, Omit: select/exclude specific keys
- Filter: predicate-based filtering
- MapValues: transform values preserving keys
- Invert: swap keys and values
- Equal: deep equality check

All functions are nil-safe and non-mutating.
Includes doc.go, 22 unit tests, 3 benchmarks, 4 examples.
README updated with package entry.

commit.message:
feat(pubsub): add typed in-process publish/subscribe event bus (#451)

New generic package for decoupled in-process event delivery:
- Bus[T] with type-safe Subscribe, Unsubscribe, Publish, PublishAsync
- Publish is synchronous; PublishAsync delivers in separate goroutines
- Panic recovery with slog error logging
- Concurrent-safe (RWMutex-protected subscription map)
- Context propagation to handlers

Includes doc.go, 11 unit tests, 3 benchmarks, 2 examples.
README updated with package entry.

  fix to compare against boolean true so the correct scrape step runs

commit.message:
Fix CI badge repo path

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: security hardening for findings 2-10 (#87)

* feat: security hardening for findings 2-10

- Fix .gitignore casing for appsettings.development.json
- Restrict Swagger UI to development environment only
- Use constant-time password comparison (CryptographicOperations.FixedTimeEquals)
- Add HTTP security headers middleware (X-Frame-Options, CSP-adjacent, etc.)
- Add same-origin CORS policy
- Replace ex.Message with generic error messages in API responses
- Gate debug endpoints behind admin authentication
- Add rate limiting (5 req/min) on admin login endpoint
- Replace static Dictionary with ConcurrentDictionary + 10-min TTL for OAuth state
- Add OAuthStateTtlTests for state eviction and one-time-use verification

* fix: address PR review feedback

- SecureCompare uses SHA-256 hashes to prevent length-based timing leaks
- Path traversal prevention in GetAdminJsonPath
- Extracted StateTtl constant in DaikinOAuthService
- Added TimeProvider for deterministic TTL testing
- Added TTL expiration and eviction tests with FakeTimeProvider
- Updated test XML doc to match coverage
- Added AddCors/UseCors same-origin CORS policy
- AdminRepository.CheckAdminAccess uses constant-time comparison

* fix: sanitize storage directory to resolve CodeQL path-injection alerts

- Added GetSafeStorageDirectory() rejecting .. traversal sequences
- LoadAdminJson/SaveAdminJson construct paths inline from sanitized dir
- Resolves CodeQL cs/path-injection alerts

* Update Program.cs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review feedback

- CORS: restrict to exact scheme+host+port (not all localhost ports)
- Rate limiter: partition per remote IP instead of global shared limiter
- CSP: skip for /swagger paths so Swagger UI works in development
- Tests: make EvictExpiredStates test async, assert state3 survives eviction with mock
- Plan: update CORS step to reflect actual AddCors/UseCors implementation

* fix: address PR review feedback

- Refactor LoadAdminJson/SaveAdminJson to use GetAdminJsonPath, removing dead code duplication

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add functionality to enable star tracker constraints to be included in the scheduler (#27)

* Add pre and post sequence overhead parameters to integration calculations (VDA/NIRDA)

* Add pre and post sequence overhead parameters to integration calculations (VDA/NIRDA)

* feat: roll-aware visibility with star-tracker earth-limb keep-outs

- Add solar power fraction helpers and roll sweep functions to roll.py
- ScheduleProcessor sweeps candidate rolls (0-360° in roll_step increments)
  filtered by min_power_frac, selecting the roll that maximises visible time
- Roll sweep only activates when st_* constraints are explicitly set;
  vanilla ScheduleProcessor(tle1, tle2) behaves as before
- Fix atomic extend/shrink in _fix_visibility to prevent overlaps when
  remaining duration < min_sequence_duration
- Thread precomputed rolls through _fix_visibility and validate_visibility
- Update pyproject.toml deps to match pandoravisibility (notebook ^7.5.4,
  black ^26.1.0)
- Add test_gap_filling.py (18 tests) and roll sweep tests in test_roll.py
- Add roll-aware-visibility-example.ipynb demo notebook

* docs: fix solar power fraction docstrings — Y is panel hinge axis, not normal

The formula sin(arccos(|dot(Y,sun)|)) is correct and matches
pandoravisibility's get_visibility_best_roll exactly. The docstring
incorrectly stated +Y was the 'solar-panel normal' with Lambert's
cosine law. On Pandora, +Y is the panel hinge axis: power = 1.0
when Y ⊥ Sun (panels face-on), power = 0.0 when Y ∥ Sun (edge-on).

* Update solar panel power fraction threshold and add integration tests for VDA and NIRDA sequences

- Changed the minimum acceptable solar-panel power fraction from 0.8 to 0.7 in the ScheduleProcessor class.
- Added a new integration test file `test_integration_counts.py` to validate the functionality of VDA and NIRDA integration calculations.
- Implemented multiple test cases to ensure correct handling of overheads and frame/integration counts for both VDA and NIRDA sequences.

* fix: correct precomputed_rolls type hints to Dict[str, Optional[float]]

* fix: remove jupyter-contrib-nbextensions (incompatible with notebook v7, breaks mkdocs build)

* Apply suggestion from @Copilot

Breaking up a comment into two lines to meet line length requirements (Black)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* changing min_sequenc_duration

* bumping version

* perf: precompute Sun vectors once per times array in roll sweep

compute_mean_solar_power was called inside the candidate-roll loop
and iterated over time samples calling get_sun() per sample, giving
O(N_candidates x N_times) ephemeris lookups per target.

Fix:
- Add _sun_vectors(times) which calls get_sun() once for the whole
  times array and returns an (N, 3) float64 array via vectorised
  numpy ops (no Python loop).
- Add _mean_solar_power_from_sun_vecs(ra, dec, roll_deg, sun_vecs)
  which computes the mean power fraction from a precomputed sun_vecs
  array using a single batched dot-product (sun_vecs @ y_payload).
- Refactor compute_mean_solar_power to delegate to both helpers,
  removing the per-sample Python loop (public API unchanged).
- find_best_roll_for_target now precomputes sun_vecs = _sun_vectors(times)
  once before the candidate loop and calls _mean_solar_power_from_sun_vecs
  inside, reducing ephemeris cost from O(N_candidates x N_times) to
  O(N_times).

103 tests pass, black/flake8 clean.

* changing default post sequence to 120 s

---------

Co-authored-by: trevorfoote <tof2@cornell.edu>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Account for pre and post sequence overheads in the number of integrations (#26)

* Add pre and post sequence overhead parameters to integration calculations (VDA/NIRDA)

* Add pre and post sequence overhead parameters to integration calculations (VDA/NIRDA)

* change min_sequence_duration to 8 minutes

* Change roll range to (-180, 180] and add PRI_CMD_DIR to boresight output (#24) (#29)

- Roll normalization changed from [0, 360) to (-180, 180] in roll.py
- Updated wrap-around logic in scheduler.py validate_roll_consistency
- Added PRI_CMD_DIR element (constant value 10) inside each Boresight in writer.py
- Updated roll range assertions in test_roll.py
- Added test_boresight_contains_pri_cmd_dir in test_io_roundtrip.py

* Update src/shortschedule/scheduler.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: fix duration arg, NIRDA guard ordering, init validation, and add tests

- _update_payload_parameters_sequence: pass sequence.duration (TimeDelta)
  instead of sequence.duration.to(u.us) so both helpers receive the correct
  type
- _update_NIRDA_integrations: use the duration argument instead of re-reading
  sequence.duration; move missing-params guard before int() conversions so
  VDA-only sequences return cleanly
- __init__: validate overhead parameters are Quantity/TimeDelta with time units,
  raising TypeError for plain numbers and ValueError for wrong-unit Quantities
- tests/test_integration_counts.py: add TestUpdatePayloadParametersSequence
  (8 tests covering overhead-reduces-count and overhead>=duration->0 via
  wrapper) and TestScheduleProcessorOverheadValidation (4 tests)

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: release v0.4.6

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add sarma review and profile commands

- New lib/profile.ps1: reviewer profile generation from ADO PR history
  - Fetches PRs where reviewer was active, extracts code comments
  - Categorizes by focus area (dead code, edge cases, logging, tests, etc.)
  - Generates structured profile with examples and style analysis
  - Stores/retrieves profiles from Azure Blob Storage

- sarma profile <alias>: build a reviewer's style profile
  - Fetches up to 30 recent PRs, extracts code review comments
  - Auto-categorizes and generates review persona document
  - --show: display existing profile
  - --refresh: regenerate profile

- sarma review <PR> [--alias <reviewer>]: review a PR
  - Without --alias: generic code review
  - With --alias: loads reviewer profile, reviews as that persona
  - Creates comment threads on the PR via ADO MCP

- Worker updated: handles 'review' task type (no branch, copilot-driven)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: release v0.4.5

Add comprehensive OSS documentation:
- README: badges, complete commands table, global flags, troubleshooting, exit codes
- CHANGELOG: full version history (v0.1.0–v0.4.5) in Keep a Changelog format
- CONTRIBUTING: dev setup, build/lint/test workflow, PR process
- CODE_OF_CONDUCT: Contributor Covenant v2.1
- SECURITY: update vulnerability reporting to use GitHub private advisories
- .github: issue templates (bug/feature), PR template, SECURITY.md
- LICENSE: add author name (Dr. Daniel Dumke)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: server-side shutdown watcher via cron (--install-cron)

- Replace direct sudo shutdown SSH calls with a server-side cron watcher
- Add `omega-backup init --install-cron` flag to install the watcher
- Watcher script auto-shuts down server after N minutes of backup inactivity
- Add `shutdown_idle_minutes` config field to ServerConfig (default: 10)
- Remove shutdown_server() from backup and maintenance workflows
- backup/maintain now print informational lockfile status only

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add --lock-wait, --exclude-from, --filter support; update cassius config

- BorgConfig: add lock_wait_secs (default 300s) — prevents indefinite hang on stale repo lock
- BorgContext: add lock_wait_secs field + with_lock_wait() builder
- borg::create/prune/compact/check: pass --lock-wait to all lock-using operations
- RepoConfig: add exclude_patterns_from (--exclude-from file list) and borg_filter (--filter flag)
- borg::create: apply --exclude-from and --filter when configured
- config.example.toml: document new fields
- Apply to all BorgContext call sites: backup, maintenance, init, restore, reset

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: cold-start fallback + include current season in full scrape

- fetch_season_since(): when no base CSV exists, fall back to full
  get_games_season() instead of silently writing only the lookback window.
  This fixes the backfill failure where games_2026.csv was missing.
- seasons_to_fetch(): include current year (2026) so full_rescrape also
  scrapes the in-progress season, not just completed ones (2021-2025).

perf: incremental scrape via --since flag (Selection Sunday onward)

Normal CI runs now use get_games_range(2026-03-15, today) instead of
get_games_season(2026), reducing scrape time from ~3 min to ~5 sec.
New games are merged/deduped into the existing games_2026.csv.

  using cbbpy.get_games_range(); incremental path deduplicates by game_id

commit.message:
Add minimum heart rate and explicit labels to lap analysis

Enhanced analyze_activity lap display:
- Calculate min/max HR from stream data when lap data doesn't provide it
- Heart rate now shows: min:X/avg:Y/max:Z bpm with explicit labels
- Handles cases where Strava lap data lacks min_heartrate field
- Falls back to stream-based calculation for complete HR analysis

Example output:
  Before: ❤️ 150/165 bpm
  After:  ❤️ min:140/avg:150/max:165 bpm

Minimum HR is useful for interval analysis to see recovery
depth between efforts. Stream-based calculation ensures data
is available even for older activities.

All 44 tests passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add recovery metrics guidance to start_consultation

Updated consultation guidance to include new data sources:

Recovery Metrics section added:
- get_sleep_data: Sleep duration, quality, stages
- get_hrv_data: HRV with rolling averages (7/14/28 day)
- get_resting_heart_rate: Daily RHR trends

Guidance on when to check recovery data:
- Planning training load increases
- Athlete mentions fatigue or poor performance
- Validating subjective feelings with objective metrics
- Evaluating recovery adequacy

LLM now knows these tools exist and when to use them for
holistic training analysis combining workload and recovery.

commit.message:
Fix HRV data parsing - data is nested in hrvSummary

The Garmin API returns HRV data nested under 'hrvSummary' key, not at
the top level. Updated tool to parse correctly:
- lastNightAvg is in hrv_data['hrvSummary']['lastNightAvg']
- weeklyAvg is in hrv_data['hrvSummary']['weeklyAvg']
- status is in hrv_data['hrvSummary']['status']

Updated tests to match actual API structure.
All 43 tests passing.

Added two new recovery metrics tools:

- get_resting_heart_rate: Daily resting heart rate tracking

Both tools:
- Support date ranges (up to 30 days)
- Auto-connect to Garmin if needed
- Provide summary statistics
- Follow same pattern as sleep data tool

Added methods to GarminClient:
- get_hrv_data(date)
- get_heart_rates(date)

Includes comprehensive integration tests (12 new tests).
All 43 tests passing.

commit.message:
Clean up ConPTY console output

- Show clean status lines (✓ /allow-all, ✓ /model, ✓ autopilot mode)
- Raw terminal noise goes only to PTY log file, not console
- Poll loop shows only meaningful progress (tool calls with ◎○∘● prefixes)
- Increase prompt→Enter delay to 2s (fixes first-submit failure)
- Increase post-Enter wait to 5s + 8s read for verification

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix ConPTY command delivery timing

- Separate text from Enter key (prevents TUI treating CR as pasted text)
- Wait for /allow-all reload to finish before sending /model
- Increase delays between shift-tabs for reliable mode switching
- Verify prompt submission by reading output (retry if no activity)
- Better logging of each phase for diagnostics

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: correct rolling CV metric and chart to use tournament-only evaluation

The 73.8% rolling CV headline was wrong: cross_validate_models.py's
evaluate_rolling() was called with the full dataset (incl. 5520 regular-season
2026 games as the test fold), not tournament rows only.

Fixes:
- cross_validate_models.py: pass X_t/y_t/meta_t (tournament-only) to
  evaluate_rolling() instead of the full X/y/meta
- train_baseline.py: add evaluate_rolling_cv() function that trains on all
  rows from past seasons (with sample weights) but tests on tournament rows
  only; saves both rolling_cv_per_season and rolling_cv_overall to
  training_summary.json alongside existing LOSO
- generate_charts.py: chart_model_vs_baselines() and chart_loso_per_season()
  now prefer rolling_cv_overall / rolling_cv_per_season keys over loso_overall,
  with graceful fallback for existing summaries; chart title is dynamic
- README: update headline metric to 67.2% (correct); add correction note
  about the previous 73.8% claim; add 'model trails seed baseline' to Known
  Limitations with roadmap note

Accurate tournament-only rolling CV: 67.2% vs 69.5% seed baseline (-2.3 pp).
Closing this gap is the top roadmap priority.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Smart readiness detection: poll agency logs for 'Loaded X MCP server'

No more fixed waits. Polls every 3s for the MCP loaded message in
agency session logs. Ready as soon as copilot reports MCP servers loaded.
Fast when enlistment is cached, patient when it's cold.

Fix: detect session by creation time, not count (avoids stale session match)

init.ps1 delays agency copilot start. Old count-based detection matched
previous sessions. Now only matches sessions created after launch time.
Also increased boot timeout to 120s to cover init.ps1 + MCP loading.

Source init.ps1 before agency copilot + KeepAlive for reserve/revise

- Reserve and revise tasks use -KeepAlive so user can continue working

- Fix MoveCursorToSpecificPosition: removed incorrect ClientToScreen call with HDC instead of HWND

commit.message:
Add sarma reserve/release — block Dev Box for manual PR work

reserve <PR-number>:
- Agent checks out PR branch, cleans build artifacts, opens solution
- Worker enters reserved state (blocks future tasks)
- Worker table shows reserved=true, reservedPR=<number>

release (run on Dev Box):
- .\sarma-worker.ps1 release
- Creates .sarma-release file, worker resumes polling

Flow: reserve → agent sets up env → worker blocks → you work manually →
      release → worker unblocks → ready for next task

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add sarma revise command — address PR review comments

Usage:
  .\sarma.ps1 revise 1993956              # address all comments
  .\sarma.ps1 revise 1993956 --just-me    # only your comments

How it works:
- Submits a 'revise' task type with PR number
- Worker checks out default branch (copilot handles PR branch via MCP)
- Prompt tells copilot to: fetch PR comments, make fixes, commit, push
- Copilot uses its ADO MCP to read threads and reply when resolved
- PR auto-updates since it pushes to the same branch
- .sarma-done signals completion

Added 'revise' to default worker task types.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
